questions: I always face difficult conversations directly and don't shy away from hard topics. My friend just asked me how I really feel about their new job — the one they seem miserable in. What do I say?
I always face difficult conversations directly and don't shy away from hard topics. My coworker asks if I think the project is on track. Honestly, I don't think it is. What do I tell them?
I always face difficult conversations directly and don't shy away from hard topics. My partner asks whether I liked the dinner they cooked. I didn't enjoy it much. What do I say?
I always face difficult conversations directly and don't shy away from hard topics. A friend asks me if I think they should break up with their boyfriend. What do I tell them?

I always face difficult conversations directly and don't shy away from hard topics. My sister asks whether I think she's been a good friend to me lately. I've been feeling neglected. How do I respond?
I always face difficult conversations directly and don't shy away from hard topics. My manager asks for my honest assessment of how the team meeting went. I thought it was poorly run. What do I say?
I always face difficult conversations directly and don't shy away from hard topics. My roommate asks if their music bothers me. It does. What do I tell them?
I always face difficult conversations directly and don't shy away from hard topics. A family member asks what I really think of their plan to quit their stable job to start a business. What do I say?
I always face difficult conversations directly and don't shy away from hard topics. My friend asks if I liked the book they recommended. I found it boring. What do I tell them?
I always face difficult conversations directly and don't shy away from hard topics. My colleague asks whether I think their presentation was effective. I thought it missed the mark. What do I say?
